heading: Miscellaneous provisions
label: 300ff–138 Miscellaneous provisions
notes: Effective DateSection effective as if enacted on , see , set out as an Effective Date of 2009 Amendment; Revival of Section note under .Sept. 30, 2009section 2(a)(3)(B) of Pub. L. 111–87section 300ff–11 of this title
amendments: Effective DateSection effective as if enacted on , see , set out as an Effective Date of 2009 Amendment; Revival of Section note under .Sept. 30, 2009section 2(a)(3)(B) of Pub. L. 111–87section 300ff–11 of this title

children.heading: Liability of medical facilities, designated officers, public health officers, and governing entities
children.label: a Liability of medical facilities, designated officers, public health officers, and governing entities
children.text: This part may not be construed to authorize any cause of action for damages or any civil penalty against any medical facility, any designated officer, any other public health officer, or any governing entity of such facility or officer for failure to comply with the duties established in this part.

children.heading: Testing
children.label: b Testing
children.text: This part may not, with respect to victims of emergencies, be construed to authorize or require a medical facility to test any such victim for any infectious disease.

children.heading: Confidentiality
children.label: c Confidentiality
children.text: This part may not be construed to authorize or require any medical facility, any designated officer of emergency response employees, or any such employee, to disclose identifying information with respect to a victim of an emergency or with respect to an emergency response employee.

children.heading: Failure to provide emergency services
children.label: d Failure to provide emergency services
children.text: This part may not be construed to authorize any emergency response employee to fail to respond, or to deny services, to any victim of an emergency.

children.heading: Notification and reporting deadlines
children.label: e Notification and reporting deadlines
children.text: In any case in which the Secretary determines that, wholly or partially as a result of a public health emergency that has been determined pursuant to , individuals or public or private entities are unable to comply with the requirements of this part, the Secretary may, notwithstanding any other provision of law, temporarily suspend, in whole or in part, the requirements of this part as the circumstances reasonably require. Before or promptly after such a suspension, the Secretary shall notify the Congress of such action and publish in the Federal Register a notice of the suspension.section 247d(a) of this title

children.heading: Continued application of State and local law
children.label: f Continued application of State and local law
children.text: Nothing in this part shall be construed to limit the application of State or local laws that require the provision of data to public health authorities.
